title: Winter Hill still not fully recovered from moor fire eight years ago

input.lead: Winter Hill, near Bolton, is still recovering years after a wildfire, according to the Wildlife Trust
input.summary: Winter Hill, near Bolton, is still recovering years after a wildfire, according to the Wildlife Trust
input.evidence_text: A moorland hit by a devastating wildfire eight years ago has still not fully recovered The summer 2018 blaze on Winter Hill near Bolton lasted 42 days and took about 100 firefighters to control and put out While some of the landscape has recovered, Alan Wright, from The Wildlife Trust for Lancashire, Manchester and North Merseyside, said the impact was still visible He said: "The hill was completely black and this whole hill behind us was on fire. And it's not quite got the same kind of vegetation [now]." He added: "Any time there's a fire it will kill lots and lots of fledglings, it will kill lots of mammals, it will kill lots of insects, and then it will create areas which are just no-go areas for years to come because there are no food plants." Wright said that despite the long-term damage, a lot of work has been done to help re-grow plants and make the environment one in which wildlife could thrive again

title: Man charged over guns found buried in Eccles garden

input.lead: A 57-year-old man is charged with a range of weapons and drug offences, Greater Manchester Police says
input.summary: A 57-year-old man is charged with a range of weapons and drug offences, Greater Manchester Police says
input.evidence_text: Man charged over guns found buried in Eccles garden - BBC News Image caption, Heavy machinery was used to dig up the cache of weapons buried in the garden in Eccles By Ewan Gawne North West Published 22 July 2026 A man has been charged with gun offences after a stash of weapons and ammunition was found buried in a back garden. The 12-day search of a home on Crowngreen Road, Eccles, uncovered a shotgun, handgun, machete and ammunition by digging up the lawn, Greater Manchester Police said. David White, of Crowngreen Road, has been charged with two counts of possession of a firearm, one count of possession of ammunition, possession of an offensive weapon in a private place and possession of Class B drugs. The 57-year-old was due to appear at Manchester Magistrates Court earlier. GMP said the charges related to a warrant executed on 8 July, when officers began their search. title: Oasis overtake The Beatles in all-time UK album chart

input.summary: The Gallagher brothers surpass their idols on a list published for the 70th anniversary of the album chart

title: Did Andy Burnham really fail to tackle homelessness as mayor?

input.lead: Critics say he failed to meet his 2020 pledge, but others say he deserves credit for his leadership
input.summary: Critics say he failed to meet his 2020 pledge, but others say he deserves credit for his leadership
input.evidence_text: Andy Burnham has twice pledged to end rough sleeping - when he was elected mayor of Greater Manchester and again in front of 10 Downing Street on Monday, in his first speech as prime minister His promise as mayor to end rough sleeping by 2020 appeared successful at first, with figures falling from 268 people sleeping on the street in 2017, to 90 in 2021 But the dip also coincided with a national effort from councils to bring rough sleepers inside during the Covid-19 pandemic, and the figure has begun to rise again alongside hikes in the cost of living Some have criticised Burnham's failure to keep his promise as mayor, while others say 'the story in Manchester is positive' There were high-profile flashpoints in the city during his time as mayor, including the clearance of tents around St Peter's Square Last year, Manchester City Council said refugees were particularly drawn to Manchester as a "friendly global city", but many were falling into rough sleeping as support services grew overloaded with rising demand

title: Why voting method change matters in mayoral poll

input.lead: Why the Supplementary Vote may allow voters to vote with their heads and their hearts
input.summary: Why the Supplementary Vote may allow voters to vote with their heads and their hearts
input.evidence_text: The Labour Party could benefit most from the return of the Supplementary Votes (SV) system in the Greater Manchester mayoral race, a polling expert has said Voters in the region head to the polls on 30 July and will be able to select a first and second choice of candidate as SV comes into replace the first-past-the-post (FPTP) system used at the last election A number of candidates have already declared for the contest , triggered after Andy Burnham's successful campaign to become the MP of Makerfield, leaving the post he has held since 2017 vacant Prof Jonathan Tonge of the University of Liverpool said Labour could pick up second preference votes from Greens and Lib Dems, while Reform could be less likely to see Restore voters put them as second preference due to "bad blood" between the party leaders The system was used in the first two mayoral elections, but was scrapped by the last Conservative government in favour of FPTP system, before it was reintroduced again by Labour Under SCV, candidates need to win more than 50% of the vote in the first round to be come mayor or the top two candidates go into a run-off where second preference votes from eliminated candidates are counted

title: What is the Greater Manchester mayor and what do they do?

input.lead: Greater Manchester goes to the polls on 30 July, but what powers does the region's mayor hold?
input.summary: Greater Manchester goes to the polls on 30 July, but what powers does the region's mayor hold?
input.evidence_text: Andy Burnham's victory in the Makerfield by-election means he has to step down as Greater Manchester mayor Burnham has been mayor since the role was first created in 2017 and the race to become his successor will be fiercely contested by all involved But what is the role they will be fighting for? The role of mayor was created as part of a law passed in 2016, one of a number of big pieces of devolution legislation brought in by the Conservatives after taking power in 2010 In a 2012 referendum, Manchester rejected having a directly-elected city mayor, along with eight other major cities, but the decision to have a regional or metropolitan mayor was directly negotiated between the government and the 10 Greater Manchester councils Andy Burnham, who left Parliament to stand for the role, was chosen as Labour's candidate for the first mayoral election in 2017 and won, taking 63.4% of the vote

title: Olympic medal and large bear figure among unusual police seizures

input.lead: Alongside gold and designer watches, police say criminals have been making some unusual investments
input.summary: Alongside gold and designer watches, police say criminals have been making some unusual investments
input.evidence_text: Olympic medal and large bear figure among unusual police seizures - BBC News Image caption, Money launderers use the purchase of unusual items to disguise their transactions, police say By Phil McCann North West Published 22 July 2026 Boxing belts, an Olympic medal and a giant bear figure are all amongst items that have been confiscated from criminals in Greater Manchester. A record amount of money, totalling £18.2m, was recovered from criminals across Greater Manchester from April 2025 to April 2026, according to police. As well as the cash, police also seized items, from designer watches and gold to more unusual items among the haul. BBC North West Tonight visited the Greater Manchester Police's evidence store to take a look at the wide-ranging collection of confiscated goods, from sports memorabilia to rare collectible toys. Image caption, The items have all been subject to separate court hearings so they could be confiscated Staff showed the BBC a signed and framed Floyd Mayweather boxing belt, a signed and framed football shirt from former Manchester City star João Cancelo and an Olympic gold medal and cap, also signed and mounted. "Money launderers have got to try and find way to disguise the proceeds of their crime, so they will invest in some quite unusual items", Det Supt Andrew Buckthorpe said. Image caption, Signed and framed sporting memorabilia included a signed swimming cap and medal from Olympian James Guy Money from the confiscated assets and forfeited cash will go into the government's Asset Recovery Incentivisation Scheme, where 50% is kept by the government but the rest is distributed back to the police force area where the crime took place. The force said their share of the money would be spent on community policing or handed to community organisatio

title: Son who killed his mum in Macclesfield home given hospital order

input.lead: Psychiatric evidence indicated Simon Carter had paranoid schizophrenia, prosecutors say
input.summary: Psychiatric evidence indicated Simon Carter had paranoid schizophrenia, prosecutors say
input.evidence_text: A man who killed his "hardworking" mother at her Macclesfield home has been given a hospital order with no fixed end date Simon Norman Carter, 50, of no fixed address, pleaded guilty to the manslaughter of Gwyneth Carter, 72, on the grounds of diminished responsibility Her family remembered her as strong and protective, with a laugh people would not forget The Crown Prosecution Service said psychiatric evidence indicated Carter was suffering from paranoid schizophrenia At Chester Crown Court on 20 July, Judge Everett imposed a hospital order under section 37 of the Mental Health Act 1983, with a restriction order under section 41 Cheshire Police said Carter, who had been living in Torquay, had been estranged from his mother for 19 years when he arrived unannounced at her Pearson Street home on 12 July 2025

title: Boy, 15, detained after man found dead in Bolton

input.lead: A 15-year-old boy has been detained under the Mental Health Act as police investigate
input.summary: A 15-year-old boy has been detained under the Mental Health Act as police investigate
input.evidence_text: A 15-year-old boy has been detained after a man was found dead at a home in Bolton A murder investigation has been launched after 50-year-old man was discovered at a house on Gray Street, on 30 June A 15-year-old boy has been detained under the Mental Health Act and will be questioned, police said Police believe it to be an isolated incident - adding there is "no wider risk to the local community" "Our thoughts remain firmly with the man's family and friends," Detective Chief Inspector Louise Edwards said. "Following new information coming to light, we are now carrying out enquiries to establish the full circumstances surrounding the events of the day he died." She added: "There is no information to suggest there is any wider risk to the public, and we are not seeking anyone else in relation to this incident."

input.evidence_text: The stars of Christopher Nolan's new blockbuster The Odyssey were given a rare chance to see a fragment of the oldest surviving book-form copy of Homer's famous epic poem Part of a 1,700-year-old copy, kept at Manchester's John Rylands Library was taken by researchers to the film's London premiere The fragment, written in ancient Greek by what was thought to be an Egyptian scribe and his teacher, is the oldest existing version of the poem arranged into a book Prof Christopher Pressler, part of the University of Manchester team tasked with preserving the work, said actor Tom Holland was "super interested" when the cast were shown the text at a press junket in New York He said: "There was some mind-blowing moments for people realising that this document is almost 2,000 years-old and of course the poem is 1,000 years older than that." When the national tabloid sent a reporter down to check it out, he reported that it was riddled with drug crime and prostitution The Sun’s piece describes the site as “Hooker Alley” and “an area popular with sex workers and homeless junkies”, where addicts can score crack for £20 and “shoot up heroin and smoke crack in the streets” And it’s a narrative echoed by Reform UK mayoral candidate Sian Astley, who posted about the area on Facebook. “The fact that Burnham’s northern ‘nerve centre’ may be only a few hundred yards from a drug hotspot tells us all we need to know about drug crime under Labour,” she sai View 6 Images Ancoats (Image: Manchester Evening News) But when our local democracy reporter Charlotte Hall ventured down there to chat to residents, she found quite a different perspective. Among the artisan bakeries, natural wine shops, bobbing houseboats, sausage dogs and yorkiepoos, she found Tom Bennion “I can’t say I’ve seen a lot of crime in the area - it’s more the kind of place where people take their kids out, walk their dogs, that kind of thing,” he said. “Mind you, I don’t hang around here at night. Maybe it all changes.”

Items such as power banks must not be placed in checked-in luggage stowed in the aircraft hold but be taken into the cabin as Incorrectly packed lithium batteries are increasingly causing delays and cancellations due to bags having to be off-loaded. input.evidence_text: Salford Tributes as ex-MP and 'Salford legend' Terry Lewis dies aged 90 Terry Lewis represented Worsley in Salford for two decades Comments News Neal Keeling and Paul Britton Reporter 13:18, 22 Jul 2026 Updated 13:51, 22 Jul 2026 View 2 Images Terry L Tributes are being paid to a respected former MP and 'Salford legend' who has died aged 90. Terry Lewis was elected as the Labour MP for Worsley in Salford in 1983 - a staunch socialist whose constituency included Salford's strongest Tory enclave He retired at the 2005 general election and was succeeded by Labour's Barbara Keeley. Mr Lewis, who was 69 when he retired from politics, once held the record for voting more than any other Labour MP against his own government, run at the time by Tony Blair After being a member for 44 years he quit the Labour Party in 2010 in disgust, saying it was close to 'Thatcherism', but rejoined in 2015 when Jeremy Corbyn became leader Mr Lewis, a born and bred Salfordian from Ordsall, served Labour as a councillor in Bolton and as a party worker before winning a seat in Parliament. The son of a dock worker, he was deputy leader of Bolton Council from 1978 to 1983 It's understood Mr Lewis, known as a well-respected as a hard-working constituency MP, passed away on Monday evening

input.summary: Friday 19 June 2026 at 1:43pm Andy Burnham has been mayor of Greater Manchester for almost a decade. Credit: PA Images Andy Burnham’s historic victory in the Makerfield by-election sets off a rapid chain reaction for both Greater Manchester and the wider UK political landscape A
input.evidence_text: Friday 19 June 2026 at 1:43pm Andy Burnham has been mayor of Greater Manchester for almost a decade. Credit: PA Images Andy Burnham’s historic victory in the Makerfield by-election sets off a rapid chain reaction for both Greater Manchester and the wider UK political landscape A metro mayor cannot simultaneously serve as an MP, a clear legal and electoral process is now underway to find someone new to take on the role Andy Burnham has won a seat as an MP - but can he still be the Mayor of Greater Manchester? No. Andy Burnham cannot be mayor and a member of parliament. He had seven days - from the moment of his election - to resign from the post which he has held for almost a decade He handed in his resignation on 19 June - triggering the process of the by-election Burnham cannot do both jobs because the Mayor of Greater Manchester holds Police and Crime Commissioner (PCC) powers and UK law strictly dictates that the office holder cannot simultaneously serve as an MP

title: Manchester builds more homes than Greater London - About Manchester

input.lead: Manchester saw more homes built in the last year (4,766) than all the 32 Greater London boroughs combined (4,261) a report to the Council’s Communities and Equalities Scrutiny Committee heard today (Weds 22 July).
input.summary: Manchester saw more homes built in the last year (4,766) than all the 32 Greater London boroughs combined (4,261) a report to the Council’s Communities and Equalities Scrutiny Committee heard today (Weds 22 July). The significant achievement marks another record-breaking year as
input.evidence_text: Manchester saw more homes built in the last year (4,766) than all the 32 Greater London boroughs combined (4,261) a report to the Council’s Communities and Equalities Scrutiny Committee heard today (Weds 22 July) The significant achievement marks another record-breaking year as part of the Council’s ambitious housing strategy up to 2032, which also saw more affordable homes built than at any point since the late 1990s with 901 affordable tenure homes completed – of which around half (439) Manchester is also bucking the national trend for affordable housing starts recording an increase of 12% since 2022/23 And the city centre also saw the largest ever delivery of affordable in the city’s history over the last year with 259 completions, helping to diversify the city centre housing market and help more Manchester people have more choice about where they live The Housing Strategy update shows that the city remains on track to meet its target to deliver at least 36,000 homes of all tenures by 2032, including 10,000 social, Council and genuinely affordable homes – 3,000 of which will be in the city centre 13,555 homes have already been delivered since 2022 with more than 7,800 currently under construction, and a further 20,000 homes with planning approval or in the pipeline

title: Bev Craig promises youth centre for every Greater Manchester town - About Manchester

input.lead: Labour’s candidate for Mayor of Greater Manchester today set out her Greater Manchester Youth Promise during a visit to Manchester Airport, highlighting how partnerships between education and employers can help open up opportunities for young people across the city region.
input.summary: Labour’s candidate for Mayor of Greater Manchester today set out her Greater Manchester Youth Promise during a visit to Manchester Airport, highlighting how partnerships between education and employers can help open up opportunities for young people across the city region. During
input.evidence_text: Labour’s candidate for Mayor of Greater Manchester today set out her Greater Manchester Youth Promise during a visit to Manchester Airport, highlighting how partnerships between education and employers can help open up opportunities for young people across the city region During the visit, Bev Craig met apprentices and young people who have taken part in the Airport Academy to hear about their experiences and ambitions for the future. She also visited the airport’s Aerozone, where thousands of young people each year take part in interactive sessio The visit showcased the role Manchester Airport is already playing in supporting young people and jobseekers into work through careers education, pre-employment training and apprenticeships, including tailored support for people who are furthest from the labour market Speaking during the visit, Bev outlined the practical commitments at the heart of her Greater Manchester Youth Promise, including a Youth Centre in every town in Greater Manchester and Additional support for care leavers She said that there will be a work placement for every young person by the age of 18 and will set up a Gen-Z Commission so young people are part of the solution, with their voices heard on issues including work, transport and health The Greater Manchester Youth Promise will tackle youth unemployment, break down barriers to opportunity and ensure every young person has the support they need to build a successful future

Good, because in just 17 days, we are hosting the social and political event of the summer: The Mill Mayoral Hustings ! It will take place just three days before polling day, and many believe the performance of the candidates at this event may well decide who succeeds Andy Burnham as mayor. Any slip up will be seized upon by the Millers, the most knowledgeable and influential crowd in the North In the hosting chair will be noted Burnhamologist Joshi Herrmann, founder of The Mill, and our staff writer Lucy McLaughlin. And instead of asking the candidates vague questions about "their plans for the city", Joshi and Lucy will be using The Mill's journalism to challenge the We've already booked four of the candidates so far and we're just waiting for Labour's Bev Craig and Restore's Marlon West to confirm. title: Winter Hill still not fully recovered from moor fire eight years ago

input.lead: The summer 2018 blaze on Winter Hill near Bolton lasted 42 days and took about 100 firefighters to control and put out While some of the landscape has recovered, Alan Wright, from The Wildlife Trust for Lancashire, Manchester and North Merseyside, said the impact was still visibl
input.summary: The summer 2018 blaze on Winter Hill near Bolton lasted 42 days and took about 100 firefighters to control and put out While some of the landscape has recovered, Alan Wright, from The Wildlife Trust for Lancashire, Manchester and North Merseyside, said the impact was still visibl
input.evidence_text: The summer 2018 blaze on Winter Hill near Bolton lasted 42 days and took about 100 firefighters to control and put out While some of the landscape has recovered, Alan Wright, from The Wildlife Trust for Lancashire, Manchester and North Merseyside, said the impact was still visible He said: "The hill was completely black and this whole hill behind us was on fire. And it's not quite got the same kind of vegetation [now]." He added: "Any time there's a fire it will kill lots and lots of fledglings, it will kill lots of mammals, it will kill lots of insects, and then it will create areas which are just no-go areas for years to come because there are no food plants." Wright said that despite the long-term damage, a lot of work has been done to help re-grow plants and make the environment one in which wildlife could thrive again "A lot of it is down to making sure the land is wet, and kind of creating areas which are wet, and looking after areas and making sure that the right type of vegetation is growing," he added

With hotter, drier summers becoming more common, it’s worth A wildfire is any uncontrolled vegetation fire serious enough to require an emergency response. Climate change is making our summers hotter and drier - ideal conditions for fire. When landscapes experience several consecutive days of dry weather and little or no rain, plants and soils lose moisture faster than they can recover. Vegetation becomes a more a It takes very little vegetation (fuel) for a wildfire to start and it is important to follow wildfire prevention advice issued by the Fire and Rescue Service . Most wildfires start due to human activities and are a risk to communities, emergency services as well as increasing pre All smoke is potentially harmful to your health. title: Smoke from wildfire affecting air quality in some areas

input.summary: Greater Manchester Fire and Rescue Service (GMFRS) and partner agencies continue to respond to the moorland fire near Dovestone Reservoir
input.evidence_text: Greater Manchester Fire and Rescue Service (GMFRS) and partner agencies continue to respond to the moorland fire near Dovestone Reservoir Strong winds have carried smoke across parts of Greater Manchester, including Manchester Residents should avoid areas affected by smoke wherever possible. If smoke is visible in your area, stay indoors with doors and windows closed. This is the most effective way to reduce exposure to wildfire smoke Wildfire smoke can irritate the eyes, nose, throat and lungs. Most healthy people will only experience temporary symptoms, but smoke can have a greater impact on babies and young children, older people, pregnant people, and those with existing heart or lung conditions For most people any symptoms will be temporary and will quickly disappear once you are away from the smoke. However, if symptoms persist or get worse, you should seek health advice Symptoms may include irritated eyes and airways, coughing, wheezing, shortness of breath and chest discomfort. If you have an asthma action plan or other agreed health management plan, follow it and make sure you have access to any prescribed inhalers or medication. If you don't

input.evidence_text: Renowned chef, broadcaster and Mowgli Street Food founder, Nisha Katona, is set to bring her trademark passion for flavour-packed food to Bolton Food and Drink Festival 2026 Food lovers will have the chance to see Nisha in action during demonstrations at 11am and 3pm on Monday 31 August in The Festival Hall at the Albert Halls, where she will share culinary tips, inspiring recipes and stories from her journey as one of the UK's best-loved food person The afternoon session will also include a glass of fizz to help celebrate the festival's twenty-first birthday Celebrated for introducing the vibrant flavours of Indian street food to audiences across the country through her award-winning Mowgli Street Food restaurants, Nisha has become a familiar face on television and a leading voice in modern British food culture Festivalgoers can expect an engaging and entertaining experience as Nisha showcases dishes inspired by her love of bold flavours and simple home cooking, offering plenty of inspiration for knowledgeable foodies and enthusiastic home cooks alike The demonstrations form part of a packed programme of celebrity chef appearances and special events taking place across the August Bank Holiday weekend

title: Community comes together to improve Mytham Park bowling hut - Bolton Council

input.evidence_text: A community-focused refurbishment of the bowling hut at Mytham Park is set to deliver lasting benefits for local residents Bolton Council and partners are planning major improvements to secure the future of this much-loved local asset The project will see a full refurbishment of the building and will include a new roof, replacement windows, new toilets, upgraded insulation, fixing damp and a complete mechanical and electrical upgrade including full rewiring Subject to planning permission, work will start in Autumn 2026 and finish ahead of the 2027 bowling season The scheme, funded by the District Centres Fund, forms part of the council’s wider commitment to invest in community facilities, ensuring they remain accessible, welcoming and fit for purpose for years to come The refurbishment will support the bowling club and its members by providing an improved environment that continues to bring people together, promotes wellbeing and strengthens community connections

title: Encouraging decline in youth smoking and vaping across Wigan Borough

input.lead: Wigan Borough is seeing a positive decline in the number of young people smoking and regularly vaping, according to a recent Trading Standards survey Following national concerns about youth smoking and vaping, Wigan Council has made it a priority to reduce these figures locally a
input.summary: Wigan Borough is seeing a positive decline in the number of young people smoking and regularly vaping, according to a recent Trading Standards survey Following national concerns about youth smoking and vaping, Wigan Council has made it a priority to reduce these figures locally a
input.evidence_text: Wigan Borough is seeing a positive decline in the number of young people smoking and regularly vaping, according to a recent Trading Standards survey Following national concerns about youth smoking and vaping, Wigan Council has made it a priority to reduce these figures locally and protect the health of our children and young people With more than 1,000 school children aged 14-17 included in the Trading Standards survey, the findings show the number of young people who have tried vaping dropped from around 44 per cent in 2023 to 18 per cent in 2026 The survey, carried out between November 2025 and February 2026, also highlights that smoking is at an all-time low, with around 97 per cent reporting to have never tried smoking Councillor Keith Cunliffe, Deputy Leader of Wigan Council and cabinet portfolio holder for adult social care and health, said: “I’m really pleased to see smoking and vaping rates among young people in Wigan Borough continuing to fall “Reducing tobacco and nicotine use among young people is one of the most effective ways to prevent long-term ill health and reduce health inequalities in our communities

title: Marina Abramović: Balkan Erotic Epic | Aviva Studios

input.lead: Experience the raw, unfiltered power of Balkan folklore in a new immersive show from the world’s most famous performance artist Balkan Erotic Epic is a bold new performance work created by the iconic artist Marina Abramović .
input.summary: Experience the raw, unfiltered power of Balkan folklore in a new immersive show from the world’s most famous performance artist Balkan Erotic Epic is a bold new performance work created by the iconic artist Marina Abramović . Regarded as the world’s most influential performance a
input.evidence_text: Experience the raw, unfiltered power of Balkan folklore in a new immersive show from the world’s most famous performance artist Balkan Erotic Epic is a bold new performance work created by the iconic artist Marina Abramović . Regarded as the world’s most influential performance artist, Abramović’s transformative work has reached millions of people – from opening the Pyramid Stage at Glastonbury Festival t A four-hour ritual where ancient myth meets performance art, Balkan Erotic Epic explores the eroticism, spirituality and traditions of Abramović’s homeland through 13 visceral scenes Brought to life by a cast of over 70 performers, dancers, musicians and singers, the experience unfolds across Aviva Studios. Audiences are free to choose their own path – with pop-up encounters punctuating the action, bringing bodies together in dance, song and ritual Watch Fertility Rite , a fevered ritual where bodies writhe against the ground in a desperate call for fertility. Witness Massaging the Breast where women gesticulate over graves to awaken the earth. In Scaring the Gods , women bare themselves to the sky to banish storms. Listen Unafraid to push the boundaries of eroticism and performance art, Balkan Erotic Epic asks its audience a timeless question: What are our bodies truly for?

NOW LIVE ON STAGE For the first time ever, a novel by John le Carré — the undisputed master of the modern spy genre whose works include Tinker, Tailor, Soldier, Spy and The Night Manager — is brought to life in a thrilling stage adaptation Starring Ralf Little ( The Royle Family , Death In Paradise ), The Spy Who Came In From The Cold is a riveting journey through the fog-shrouded terrain of Cold War espionage, deception, and moral compromise Following a sold-out run at Chichester Festival Theatre and direct from the West End, this gripping new play written by David Eldridge ( Beginning, Middle, End ) and directed by Jeremy Herrin ( Grace Pervades, People, Places & Things ) comes to LOWRY for 1 week only ​​★★★★ ​‘A tense, gripping adaptation with moody flair’ - Financial Times

title: Banner Bingo x International Mother Language Day - People's History Museum

input.evidence_text: For International Mother Language Day 2026 People’s History Museum launched Banner Bingo in an additional seven languages Alongside English, visitors can take their pick from Arabic, Chinese, French, Persian, Polish, Spanish, and Urdu as they take part in the free Family Friendly activity at the museum Banner Bingo is a quest to find a series of images featured within the colourful banners that are on display throughout the museum’s galleries. You can pick up a Banner Bingo card from the welcome desk, and after completing your search there is a small prize for taking part. Two Part of a programme of activities organised by Manchester City of Literature to celebrate International Mother Language Day The translations of Banner Bingo were provided by Peak Translations Ltd, thebigword, The Translation People and the Association of Translation Companies Are you more independent or community focused? input.evidence_text: I Am The Fly at Refuge - Manchester’s Finest I Am The Fly at Refuge Thu 23rd Jul, 2026 - Sat 26th Sep, 2026 - The Refuge I Am The Fly at Refuge The Refuge, Oxford St, Manchester M60 7HA Thu 23rd Jul, 2026 - Sat 26th Sep, 2026 Free More info Directions events Go to Manchester's Finest Facebook page Go to Manchester's Finest X profile Original screen-printed posters and photographs from Manchester’s pioneering early 1980s music scene are being brought together for I Am The Fly, a free exhibition curated by artist Matthew Rich. Created to promote the club nights, artists and venues that helped shape the city’s cultural identity, many of the works were originally flyposted and never intended to survive. Expect rare material connected to the Haçienda era, FAC51 and the wider scene that established Manchester as a global music city. Interested in more events? input.evidence_text: There’s a cruelty to Mike Rice, a wickedness. In this show Mike talks about doing psychedelics with his brother, an entanglement with a Spanish Sausage Dog named Nino, and many other such rotten adventures ‘ Ireland has yet another legendary comedian ‘ – Dan Nightingale Irish farmer’s son, and general Nasty Character Mike Rice is one of Ireland and the UK’s fastest rising comedy stars. Having started comedy in 2016 Mike has amassed a large online following from podcast and stand-up clips, regularly getting millions of views on various platforms Mike began stand-up comedy in Chicago, where within two years he was working regularly at the most prestigious clubs in the city, including The Laugh Factory. He has since returned home because his mother made him, and has been quickly rising in the Irish and European scene, play Mike brought his first solo show The Best Boy in Ireland to the Edinburgh Fringe Festival 2018, in what was a sold-out run, and performed that same show to sold-out rooms at the Perth Fringe Festival in 2019 As well as co-hosting Mike & Vittorio’s Guide to Parenting with Vittorio Angelone, and Big Mike and The Chief with Rob Moriarty, Mike has appeared multiple times on smash hit podcast Have a Word and released his first comedy special in October 2023

input.evidence_text: Manchester Digital hosted a lunchtime Tech Leader Talks on 11th June 2026, which was chaired by Pete Farrell, Technology Director at Autotrader. The panel topic was ‘Build, Buy or Rebuild at Scale’, which explored the ongoing challenges for engineers and tech leaders. The panel b A key theme throughout the discussion was recognising the signals that it is time to move on from legacy software and to modernise tech stacks. Richard said the trigger often comes from customers rather than technology itself. At Lloyds Banking Group (LBG), a move to Google Cloud Mariam pointed to developer experience and security as critical drivers for change. Systems built on older frameworks, such as early Ruby on Rails stacks, can become difficult to maintain or secure over time. She explained, “You don’t want to be running systems with outdated pack Chris explained that with Street being a relatively new business, their challenge is different - they don’t have legacy tech but they are scaling at such a pace that growth is doubling or tripling every year. This means that early architectural decisions can be constraining, but Discussion touched on bringing stakeholders on board with any upgrades or transformation. Chris and Mariam both reinforced the need for a strong narrative, effectively ‘selling’ the journey to non-technical stakeholders and bringing people on the journey with you Richard talked about pace of delivery, and said: “If you haven’t made the right decisions, the pace of delivery will change down the line. input.evidence_text: A derelict Stockport pub, which has stood empty for over two and a half years, is to be brought back to life in a £1 million revival project The Golden Hind in Offerton has been purchased by Joseph Holt, with the Manchester-based brewery committing to a seven-figure investment as it seeks to create a ‘traditional pub and lively hub for the entire community and beyond’ The Lisburne Lane venue closed in November 2023 after 56 years of trading. Public planning application documents detail a significant scope of works, including a brand-new canopied terrace, a new bar and lounge with a log burner, the installation of a traditional snug, new toilet Richard Kershaw, CEO of Joseph Holt, said: “Joseph Holt is a brewery committed to putting pubs back into the heart of local communities. It is at the core of our ethos. Since we know they play such an important role not just as places to enjoy a drink, but as welcoming spaces to “All of which will be happening once again once we complete our redevelopment of the Golden Hind. The building also includes two flexible spaces for school visits, community events, meetings, exhibitions and performances When the site opens, visitors will be able to enjoy an expanding network of accessible green spaces , orchards, community gardens, food-growing areas and nature-rich landscapes. title: Kids can claim free unlimited bus travel across Greater Manchester through summer

input.lead: Children and young people across Greater Manchester can benefit from free unlimited bus travel on the Bee Network this summer As part of the government’s Great British Summer Savings campaign, children aged 5-15 can travel for free in England, including on Bee Network bus service
input.summary: Children and young people across Greater Manchester can benefit from free unlimited bus travel on the Bee Network this summer As part of the government’s Great British Summer Savings campaign, children aged 5-15 can travel for free in England, including on Bee Network bus service
input.evidence_text: Children and young people across Greater Manchester can benefit from free unlimited bus travel on the Bee Network this summer As part of the government’s Great British Summer Savings campaign, children aged 5-15 can travel for free in England, including on Bee Network bus services, throughout August In addition, 16-year-olds in Greater Manchester will be able to activate their Our Pass one month earlier (from Sat 1 Aug instead of Tue 1 Sep), giving them an extra month of free bus and cheaper tram travel Anyone aged between 16 and 18 on Mon 31 Aug 2026 and a resident of Greater Manchester is eligible to apply for a digital Our Pass via the Bee Network app, with a one-off £10 admin fee. Once approved, Our Pass can be activated from Sat 1 Aug and used immediately Fran Wilkinson, Customer and Growth Director at Transport for Greater Manchester, said: “We’re pleased to be taking part in the Greater British Summer Savings scheme which will mean free bus travel on the Bee Network for everyone aged five to 15 in August. This will help make day “To complement this national scheme, we are allowing Our Pass to be activated one month early which means no young person in Greater Manchester will miss out. The Bee Network is all about making it easier and more affordable to get around Greater Manchester and this national sche

The neoclassical design has made it one of the city’s most-loved buildings, holding thousands of stories in its walls and on its shelves. The square itself ha From here, the tour takes you through the city’s heart, down the colourful streets of the Gay Village and the calming Sackville Gardens. It is here that you will see the monuments that honour Manchester’s role in the fight for equality and the city’s pioneering LGBTQ+ community a This guided tour is filled with monuments and tales from many moons ago. The Medieval Quarter is the perfect place to soak in all that history. It is here where you will see the oldest public library in the English-speaking world, Chetham’s Library . Housed in a beautiful sandsto During all this walking, your guide will point out local street art, hidden corners and stories you’d easily miss on your own. input.summary: Huge Sunday car boot at Bolton's Macron Stadium (BL6 6JW), Feb-Dec. 300+ stalls, £1 per car entry, catering, toilets and dog-friendly Event date: 2026-07-26 Venue: Bolton Car Boot Sale Price: £1 Booking: https://carbootdirectory.co.uk/listings/bolton-car-boot/ Status: https://sch
input.evidence_text: Bolton Car Boot Sale | Bolton Sunday Car Boot Sale | Times, Prices & Info Skip to content 210+ UK car boot sales — find one near you this weekend Type your postcode or town to find sales near you Search car boot sales About This Car Boot Sale Bolton Car Boot Sale is one of the North West's biggest weekly boot sales, held in the hardstanding car park of the Macron Stadium (formerly Reebok), Burnden Way, Horwich, Bolton BL6 6JW. It runs every Sunday from February right through to December, drawing over 300 sellers and thousands of bargain hunters each week. Sellers can arrive and set up from 6:00 AM, with buyers welcomed in from 7:00 AM. Entry costs just £1 per car for buyers. Pitches for sellers are £14 for a car, £20 for a van and £3 for a trailer, with regular and corner pitches available on request. Bring exact change to keep the queue moving, and call organiser Andrew on 07470784644 for more details. Expect hundreds of stalls across the concrete hardstanding pitches, so it goes ahead in most weather. On-site you'll find a 5-star catering service, doughnuts and coffee, ice cream and desserts, a meat auction, fresh fruit and veg, plants, and retro sweet stalls. The sale is dog-friendly, has male and female toilets, ample parking, and kids' rides and bouncy castles to keep little ones entertained. Last confirmed open: 8 June 2026. title: Rodri makes history after World Cup triumph

input.lead: Rodri makes history after World Cup triumph
input.summary: And he’s just the seventh male player in world football to win a domestic league title, World Cup, continental title with his country, the Champions League and the Ballon d’Or. The midfielder completed the remarkable feat after leading Spain to a 1-0 extra time win over Argentina
input.evidence_text: And he’s just the seventh male player in world football to win a domestic league title, World Cup, continental title with his country, the Champions League and the Ballon d’Or. The midfielder completed the remarkable feat after leading Spain to a 1-0 extra time win over Argentina in the 2026 World Cup final. And to underline his importance to La Roja’s achievement, Rodri also won the Golden Ball for player of the tournament. It’s the latest triumph of an astonishing career that has also seen him win 13 trophies in a City shirt. Since moving to the Etihad Stadium in July 2019, he has won four Premier League titles, two FA Cups and four League Cups. His greatest moment for the Club came in the 2023 Champions League final when he scored the winning goal in our 1-0 victory over Inter in Istanbul. As well as winning the UEFA Super Cup and FIFA Club World Cup with City the following year, Rodri then helped Spain to European Championship success – winning the Golden Ball for the tournament’s best player. That led him to becoming City’s first player to win the Ballon d’Or, a deserved accolade for an incredible year. Now adding a World Cup winner’s medal to his collection, the 30-year-old becomes the only footballer to ever the four biggest trophies for England-based players and the highest individual award available. Ten other players have won the World Cup, Champions League and Ballon d’Or with Rodri become the 11th and first Spaniard to do so. Germany legends Franz Beckenbauer and Gerd Muller and France great Zinedine Zidane have also won a domestic league title and the European Championships. Brazil’s Rivaldo and Ronaldinho and Argentina’s Lionel Messi have also won a domestic league title and the Copa America – the South American championship. Everyone at City would like to

input.evidence_text: 17 октября 2026 года в Brixton Jamm состоится концерт Андрея Кравченко в Лондоне Андрей Кравченко покорил сердца тысяч слушателей благодаря своему уникальному голосу, харизме и способности превращать личные истории в песни, близкие каждому. В его творчестве смешиваются романтическая лирика, современное эстрадное звучание и проникновенная искренность После серии аншлаговых выступлений на самых престижных сценах Украины, среди которых Жовтневый Дворец и Дворец “Украина”, певец продолжает свое концертное путешествие и приглашает поклонников на особую встречу в Лондоне Концерты Андрея Кравченко известны своей теплой атмосферой: здесь нет дистанции между сценой и залом, ведь каждое выступление превращается в откровенный разговор на языке музыки. Гостей ждут любимые хиты, живой звук, море эмоций и моменты, которые захочется сохранить в памяти над Важной частью творческой миссии артиста остается поддержка Украины — часть средств от концерта будет передана на помощь ВСУ! Подарите себе вечер настоящей романтики, красивой музыки и искренних чувств вместе с Андреем Кравченко

input.evidence_text: Rudy’s Pizza Napoletana has confirmed it will be bringing its relaxed dining experience and renowned Neapolitan pizzas to Salford for the first time, with a new restaurant opening in Monton village this autumn. To celebrate, the pizzeria is giving away 3,000 pizzas on the house a The new pizzeria, located at 237-239 Monton Road , will transform the former Bennetts Furnishers store that has been vacant since 2022, with work now officially underway. The spacious venue will mark Rudy’s 41st restaurant to date Since launching in Manchester’s Ancoats in 2015, Rudy’s has grown into one of the UK’s most loved pizzerias, celebrated for its light, double fermented dough and unwavering dedication to true Neopolitan craft Testament to its authenticity, Rudy’s is the largest organisation in the world to be accredited by the Associazione Verace Pizza Napoletana (AVPN), the international body that protects and promotes traditional Neapolitan pizza making Rudy’s has also been recognised among the World’s 50 Best Pizzeria, ranking 23rd globally for 2025. Each pizza, from the classic Margherita to the fiery Calabrese, is made to AVPN standards using traditional techniques and ingredients sourced directly from Italy To claim one of 3,000 complimentary pizzas, Monton locals can sign up via this link , where they will be able to choose from Rudy’s favourites such as its Cinghiale , Margherita Con Bufala, and Triple Pepperoni to enjoy at the new opening this summer
